summary: Audience‑sourced (AUD) recording with mix glitches and pitch quirks, but it captures a memorable 1978 show highlighted by “If I Had the World to Give”, “Eyes of the World” and the laid‑back jam on “Aiko”.
review: The performance is praised by most reviewers for its emotive Garcia vocals, sublime Eyes of the World, and a rare early rendition of If I Had the World to Give, while a few note low energy and sloppy moments. The recording is an audience tape that suffers from missing mix elements (Jerry’s guitar), a blown speaker, and needed pitch corrections, making it only fair in fidelity. Overall the show shines despite technical imperfections.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the musical performance highly (average ~4‑5 stars) while the detailed technical review rates the recording as fair/poor; weighting show 70% and recording 30% yields a combined rating around 3.4.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape with missing Jerry mix, blown speaker on guitar, occasional muffled sections, required pitch adjustments (+1% to match The Other One), edit cuts in SBDs, and overall less‑than‑ideal balance.

standout_songs: If I Had the World to Give
show_quality.setlist_flow: Short first set with some mix problems and a sluggish Stagger Lee, followed by a longer second set where the band recovers energy; Eyes of the World shines as the highlight while the overall momentum is uneven.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Emotive playing praised but frequently lost in the mix (blown speaker, missing from AUD), with occasional strong jam sections.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass sometimes hard to hear due to mix issues; Space segment noted as a highlight for Phil fans.
band_member_comments.Bob: Prominent slide guitar throughout, though at times it feels like a practice session rather than musical contribution.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy broke his wrist prior to the show, leading to one‑handed drumming and moments where the drums fell behind the beat.
